sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.95 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a finitely generated pro-$p 0.2ex$-group and let$g_1, ,g_n G$. Let$H$be an open subgroup of$G$, and suppose there exists a non-trivial word$w=w(X_1, ,X_n)$such that$w(a_1, ,a_n)=1$whenever$a_1 g_1H, ,a_n g_nH$(that is,$G$satisfies a coset identity\/). Does it follow that$G$satisfies some non-trivial identity? A positive answer to this question would imply that an analogue of the Tits Alte...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
